dashboard.description: Comprehensive view of Kubernetes cluster health including real-time CPU, memory, network, and storage utilization across nodes, namespaces, deployments, and pods. Monitors node health metrics, cluster events (Normal/Warning), and historical trends for troubleshooting. Designed for operators to identify resource bottlenecks, analyze workload distribution, and track infrastructure performance.

panels.description: The average CPU usage of all the nodes in the cluster.
panels.title: Cluster CPU Usage

panels.description: The memory usage in percentage of all nodes in the cluster.
panels.title: Cluster Memory Usage

panels.description: The pod usage in a cluster grouped by Node
panels.title: Pods per Node

panels.description: A list of all nodes in the cluster along with their spec. This table helps to get a quick idea about the resources in the cluster.
panels.title: Cluster Node

panels.description: This panel displays the total number of container restarts per pod and container within each namespace, correlated with the reason for the last termination event.

targets.expr:
sort_desc(sum(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total{namespace!~"kube-system|metallb-system", cluster=~"$cluster"}) by (pod, namespace)
* on(pod, namespace) group_right()
sum(kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason{namespace!~"kube-system|metallb-system", cluster=~"$cluster"}) by (pod, namespace, reason)
or sum(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total{namespace!~"kube-system|metallb-system", cluster=~"$cluster"}) by (pod, namespace)
* on(pod, namespace) group_right()
sum(kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason{namespace!~"kube-system|metallb-system", cluster=~"$cluster"}) by (pod, namespace, reason)
) > 0

panels.title: Pod Stability Overview

targets.expr: sum by(source) (count_over_time({source="fluent_bit", job="kube_events", cluster=~"$cluster"} | json | type = `Warning` [$__range])>0)

panels.title: Memory usage by namespace

panels.title: Memory usage by deployment

panels.title: CPU Usage by pod
